title: Adult education and skills funding allocations
body: "Adult education and skills funding allocations   We use this page to explain how we allocate funding for:     adult skills fund (ASF)     advanced learner loans facilities and bursaries    apprenticeships carry-in and apprenticeship non-levy extensions     We will provide updates as we move through the funding allocation cycle and issue all allocations through manage your education and skills funding.   How we calculate allocations   We explain how we calculate your funding allocations in our technical guidance when we start to issue the allocations. We calculate allocations annually using nationally consistent methodologies, taking into account the current policy position and affordability.   The general principles we typically follow when setting allocations are to:     reflect historic (lagged) delivery and current allocations to ensure that future allocations are in line with what providers can realistically earn    ensure that we treat all providers fairly and equitably    protect public funds and meet principles of public law    enable providers to respond to government priorities     Information for academic year 2025 to 2026   Our funding allocation guidance will help you to understand how we’ve calculated your funding and what data sources we have used.   We will publish calculators to help you understand your allocation value.   Allocation timeline   We will publish updates to the timeline as we deliver funding allocations. The latest news column on the timeline shows updates.      Month Activity Latest news      July and August 2024 We will issue 2024 to 2025 funding agreements/contracts to providers. Providers must digitally sign agreements and contracts on Manage your education and skills funding. We have issued the contracts.   October 2024 This is the individualised learner record (ILR) R14 deadline. We use this to calculate additional funding in line with the extra ASF review published in the ASF funding rules. The R14 data return is now closed.   December 2024 This is the ILR R04 deadline. We use this to calculate procured non-levy apprenticeship allocations for 2025 to 2026. The R04 data return is now closed.   December 2024 We aim to issue the final values for ASF (non procured) continuing learners following the completion of the 2023 to 2024 funding year. Allocations issued 10 December 2024.   February 2025 We will issue apprenticeship non-levy extension contracts for financial year 2025 to 2026. Allocations issued 6 Feb 2025.   March 2025  Apprenticeships non-levy extensionsProviders must sign their contracts by 28 March 2025 otherwise they will expire.The business case deadline for these allocations is 28 March 2025.Submit a business case to address significant R04 data errors that have affected your funding allocation.Do not submit a case to request growth. The window for these has now closed.   April 2025 We aim to complete issuing funding allocations for advanced learner loans,  ASF and ASF contract for services by the end of the month and update our funding allocations guidance. We will provide calculators and business case forms. Advanced learner loans allocations issued 3 April.ASF allocations issued 24 April 2025.   May 2025 The business case deadline for ASF and advanced learner loans allocations is the end of May 2025.
